title: Geum rubrum
intro: Geum rubrum, also known as the red avens, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, fragrant flowers in shades of pink to red. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Geum rubrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, such as gein, geissoschizine, and umbelliferone, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Geum rubrum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies for its purported health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Geum rubrum is classified as a perennial flowering plant belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Geum, which includes various species of avens. The genus Geum is further divided into several sections, with Geum rubrum being part of the section Pseudogeum. Geum rubrum is native to Europe and Western Asia, and its classification is consistent with other members of the Rosaceae family. As a member of the family Rosaceae, Geum rubrum shares characteristics with other related plants such as roses and apples.
